task_completion.description: The agent opens the main application menu from the map screen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A sidebar, bottom sheet, or full-screen menu overlay is visible on top of the main map screen in the Sygic app.

task_completion.description: The agent navigates to the Settings menu.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main 'Settings' screen of the Sygic app is displayed, showing various configuration categories.

task_completion.description: The agent opens the 'View & Units' settings category.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A sub-menu titled 'View & Units' (or similar regional/display settings) is open on the screen.

task_completion.description: The agent locates the 'Distance units' setting.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The specific setting labeled 'Distance units' is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The agent changes the distance units to a Miles-based option.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Distance units' setting is shown as set to either 'Miles/Yards' or 'Miles/Feet', or a selection dialog is open and one of these options is being selected.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Sygic GPS Navigation application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the genuine Sygic app interface, not a text editor, web browser, or mock application.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to navigate to the settings.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory shows step-by-step GUI navigation (menus opening, scrolling) rather than jumping directly to the final screen via terminal commands or intents.

integrity.description: The agent did not fake the setting change by typing into an unrelated field.
integrity.visual_evidence: The setting change occurs within the actual app settings menu, not by typing 'Miles' into a search bar, text editor, or unrelated text field.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target setting is exactly named 'Distance units' and is located in the 'View & Units' menu. Note that the official app does not have a standalone 'Miles' option; selecting either 'Miles/Yards' or 'Miles/Feet' satisfies the requirement to change the units to Miles.
